parts.title: A. K_5 triangle system: UNSAT iff c0 = 1 (closure rests on c0 = 1)

checks.name: A- and B-slot cycles are edge-disjoint and cover K_5
c0 = 0:  K_5 (H-coc) system is SATISFIABLE (no contradiction)
=> the entire f=7 (2,2) closure reduces to proving the single orientation bit  c0 = 1

parts.title: B. Orientation character eps = sgn(det): the three forcing facts

checks.name: generator Rx is orthogonal (a linear isometry)
generator Sz is orthogonal (a linear isometry)
generator Swp is orthogonal (a linear isometry)
eps(A.B) = eps(A).eps(B) on all generator products (eps is a homomorphism, no stabiliser slack to spoil it)
isometries (in the signed-permutation model) fixing the facet plane pointwise: exactly {id, reflection}
the non-identity facet-fixing isometry is the plane reflection, eps = -1
a facet-fixing isometry that swaps the two sides (opposite-side occupancy) is unique and equals the reflection
therefore the constant facet correction has eps(c) = -1, i.e. c0 = bit(eps(c)) = 1

parts.title: C. Synthesis: the forced bit and the unconditional contradiction

checks.name: forced-bit identity  bit(g_ij) = 1 + s(i) + s(j)  holds for all handedness sign choices
with the forced bit, EVERY K_5 triangle sum is odd (= 1) for EVERY handedness assignment: (H-coc) cocycle can never hold
CONCLUSION: (H-coc) is discharged -- the f=7 (2,2) sub-case is closed UNCONDITIONALLY

proof_boundary_note: The signed-permutation enumeration is an exact sanity model for the coordinate representatives; the all-isometry statement used in the proof is the standard Euclidean lemma that an isometry fixing a planar facet pointwise is either the identity or the reflection in that plane.

conclusion.mechanism: orientation character eps=sgn(det) forces bit(g_ij)=1+s(i)+s(j); K_5 non-bipartite => UNSAT
conclusion.residual: none for genuine (2,2) orientation branch; the quadrilateral-pyramid branch is discharged separately, and only the accidental-even metric residual remains in the global synthesis
